summarization.a: Adsorption equilibrium state for the (SiH₃O)₃Si–O–TiI₃* + H₂O reaction, showing H₂O binding to the apical site of the −TiI₃ group, stabilized by lone pair donation from oxygen to titanium, with a 0 K adsorption heat of 6.2 kcal/mol.
summarization.b: Transition state for the reaction, showing Ti–O bond at 1.95 Å, Ti–I bond broken (3.88 Å), partial formation of the H–I bond, an activation barrier of 20.3 kcal/mol, and slight exothermicity of 0.2 kcal/mol.

data_extraction.a:
|Atom Pair|Bond Length (Å)|Notes|
|---------|---------------|-----|
|Ti - O1|1.95|Adsorption of H2O at apical site of -TiI3 group|
|Ti - I|3.88|Bond length increased in adsorption state|
|O lone pair → Ti-O*|67.44 kcal/mol|Stabilization energy from NBO donation|
|H2O charge|+0.1 e|Charge transfer from O to Ti|
|ΔH_adsorption|6.2 kcal/mol|Heat of adsorption at 0 K|
data_extraction.b:
|Atom Pair|Bond Length (Å)|Notes|
|---------|---------------|-----|
|Ti - O (H2O)|1.95|Bonding in transition state|
|Ti - I|3.88|Bond broken in transition state|
|O lone pair → H-I*|Partial delocalization|Indicates partial O-H bond breakage and H-I formation|
|ΔH_reaction|0.2 kcal/mol|Slightly exothermic at 0 K|
|Activation Barrier|20.3 kcal/mol|From adsorption to transition state|

a.question: Does the adsorption of H₂O onto the -TiI₃ group proceed via a transition state where the Ti–I bond breaks?

b.question: How does the Ti–O bond length change from the adsorption equilibrium state to the transition state?
b.answer: It decreases from the adsorption equilibrium state to 1.95 Å in the transition state.

d.question: How can the mechanistic insights from this reaction step be applied to optimize ALD process conditions?
d.answer: Understanding the adsorption and transition state energetics helps predict the activation barriers and exothermicity of surface reactions. For ALD process design, this information guides the choice of temperature and exposure times to ensure efficient surface hydroxyl regeneration, minimize undesired side reactions, and optimize precursor dosing. It also allows estimation of rate constants for kinetic modeling of the full deposition cycle.

c.question: List the key structural and electronic changes observed during the H₂O adsorption and transition to the reaction state.
c.answer: H₂O binds to the apical site of -TiI₃, Lone pair donation from oxygen to Ti stabilizes the adsorption complex (67.44 kcal/mol), Charge transfer from H₂O to Ti (+0.1 e), Coordination geometry changes from tetrahedral to trigonal bipyramidal, Ti–I bond elongates to 3.88 Å in the transition state, Partial formation of H–I bond, O–H bond partially breaks in the transition state
